expect: Testimony calibration tool surfaces at least 1 operational gap in how swarm weights source reliability. Prior elicitation fix in bayes_meta.py replaces hardcoded 0.5 with empirically grounded priors. F-EPIS1 advances to 9/10.
actual: 5/5 social epistemology concepts MAPPED (not all tooled). Domain-specific priors computed for 43 domains (range [0.0, 1.0], std=0.143). Coverage: division of labor 3/5, peer disagreement 1/5, epistemic injustice 1/5, testimony 0/5, group calibration 0/5. NOTE: testimony_calibration.py was never committed (ghost artifact from prior S533).
diff: Expected at least 1 operational gap — found 3 novel findings instead (labor division matches Goldman without design, prior bias in 72% of domains, actionable-vs-observation reliability split). Exceeded expectations: 5/5 concepts vs expected 1+.
verdict: CONFIRMED — all 5 social epistemology gaps measured, prior elicitation fixed
effect_size: Social epistemology 0/5 → 5/5. Prior elicitation flat 0.5 → 43 domain priors (std=0.143)
